Why NCA-DCA Malawi

The NCA-DCA 2025–2029 strategic priorities align directly with the integrated programme model I designed and delivered at FISD — now a WFP national replication reference.

Economic Development & Food Security

Led USD 10.3M Cash Assistance and FFA programmes that increased household incomes by up to 500% through integrated livelihoods and market linkage.

Climate & Environmental Stewardship

Integrated watershed management, conservation agriculture, and solar irrigation — the Domwe-Mposa model broke recurring hunger within 2 years.

Fighting Inequalities & GBV

Directly supervised Social Protection & Gender Officer. Embedded gender-responsive targeting and GBV risk mitigation across all 21 distribution points.

Multi-Partner Consortium

FISD focal point coordinating with CARE International, World Vision, Plan International, Self Help Africa, and Save the Children within WFP INGO consortium.

Clement Phiri

Humanitarian Programme Specialist · Blantyre, Malawi

I am a humanitarian programme professional with 7 years of direct ECHO/WFP programme management in Malawi, reinforced by over 3 years of applied water and energy engineering expertise. I managed a USD 10.3 million ECHO portfolio reaching 13,721 beneficiaries across 6 districts with 100% PDM satisfaction and zero critical audit findings across the full 6-year programme period.

As creator of the Domwe-Mposa Integrated Livelihoods Model — now a WFP Malawi national replication reference — I broke the cycle of recurring hunger within two years through integrated watershed management, solar irrigation, VSL formation, and market linkage.

I am uniquely positioned to lead the NCA-DCA ECHO-funded Humanitarian Cash Assistance and Protection Project, having managed the identical programme structure for 7 consecutive years. I am prepared to relocate to Lilongwe immediately and available to commence at short notice.

Alignment with NCA-DCA JCP 2025–2029

ECHO Cash Assistance & Protection

7 years managing the exact programme structure NCA-DCA will implement — ECHO-funded, 12-month, Cash Assistance and Protection. Submitted reports by the 28th of every month for 6 consecutive years. Zero compliance findings.

EAM & FOCESE Partnership

As FISD focal point in WFP's multi-INGO consortium, coordinated with CARE, World Vision, Plan International, Self Help Africa, and Save the Children. Understand the value faith-based and CSO partners bring in last-mile access.

Protection Mainstreaming

Supervised Social Protection & Gender Officer. Protection embedded as an integrated pillar — not standalone — across all 21 FDPs. Established community feedback mechanisms meeting WFP AAP standards.

Technical Infrastructure

BSc Irrigation Engineering + 3 years at Davis & Shirtliff. Can conduct solar irrigation, borehole, and WASH assessments without external technical support — reducing programme costs and accelerating timelines.

WFP National Replication Model

Domwe and Mposa in Blantyre are now cited by WFP Malawi as a national replication model. Broke the cycle of recurring hunger within 2 years through integrated watershed management and livelihoods programming.

371 VSL Groups · 500% Income Growth

Established 371 Village Savings and Loan groups. Linked 2 cooperatives to PTS and Spar (Ekhaya), enabling up to 500% household income growth through market access, business training, and value chain integration.

Career Timeline

Professional Experience

Aug 2022 – Dec 2024
Davis & Shirtliff Group
Blantyre, Malawi

Malawi Representative Technical Engineer

Water & Energy Engineering

Africa's leading water and energy solutions company — solar irrigation, boreholes, water treatment, renewable energy, and generators.

  • Designed and supplied solar-powered irrigation systems, borehole equipment, water treatment solutions, and renewable energy installations across Malawi — directly applicable to humanitarian programme infrastructure.
  • Provided technical consultation for agricultural, community, and institutional clients on water access and energy infrastructure, maintaining active engagement with the humanitarian and resilience sector.
  • Applied expertise in solar irrigation, borehole development, and WASH systems — reducing dependency on external technical specialists in humanitarian programme contexts and lowering programme delivery costs.
  • Maintained active sector networks across water, energy, and food systems, sustaining continued professional engagement with the humanitarian ecosystem in Malawi.
Sep 2016 – Aug 2022
Foundation for Irrigation & Sustainable Development (FISD)
Blantyre, Malawi

Humanitarian Programme Manager (Programs Manager)

WFP · ECHO · USAID · DFID · BMZ — USD 10.3M Portfolio

Led the full programme cycle of ECHO-funded WFP Cash Assistance and Protection projects — the identical programme structure NCA-DCA will implement.

  • Programme Management: Led planning and implementation of ECHO-funded cash assistance and protection project across 21 field sites. Overseeing all programmatic, financial, and administrative aspects ensuring on-time, on-scope, and on-budget delivery of USD 10.3M portfolio.
  • Consortium Coordination: Served as FISD's focal point across WFP multi-INGO consortium — coordinated with CARE International, World Vision, Plan International, Self Help Africa, Save the Children, and other WFP INGO partners. Managed joint workplans, reporting timelines, and compliance processes.
  • Donor Reporting: Submitted comprehensive, high-quality narrative and financial reports by the 28th of every month across the full 6-year period. Zero compliance findings or late submissions. Coordinated consolidated overviews for Head of Programme at national level.
  • Risk Management: Developed and maintained risk analysis matrix with WFP Senior Humanitarian Policy Officer. Led risk management, mitigation processes, and facilitated risk awareness training with programme teams.
  • Community Assessments: Facilitated CBPP, SLP, and 3PA holistic assessments at TA and GVH level across Blantyre, Mangochi, Zomba, Chikwawa, Balaka, and Nsanje. Supervised 21 Food/Cash Distribution Points with 2 monitoring visits per day Mon–Thu.
  • Protection Mainstreaming: Directly supervised Social Protection and Gender Officer. Embedded protection as an integrated cross-cutting pillar with gender-responsive targeting, GBV risk mitigation, and community feedback mechanisms at all 21 FDPs meeting WFP AAP standards.
  • Market Systems: Conducted monthly market studies to inform WFP transfer modality selection (food vs. cash). Used ODK and KoboToolbox for data collection and KPI tracking. Linked 2 cooperatives to PTS and Spar (Ekhaya) — up to 500% income growth.
  • Team Leadership: Supervised R4 Coordinator, SAMs Coordinator, 4 Project Officers, and 9 Field Monitoring Assistants. Built team capacity across irrigation, forestry, land resources, public works, agriculture, nutrition, and education.
  • Technical Infrastructure: Engineering design and construction supervision of 122 ha irrigation infrastructure (Bakasala 100ha, Domwe 12ha, Mposa 10ha). Developed 2 solar irrigation systems + 21 shallow wells — 80% improvement in clean water access.
Jan 2016 – Sep 2016
FISD
Blantyre, Malawi

Irrigation Engineer / Programme Officer

IRMP · Food & Cash Distribution
  • Conducted community mobilisation, CBPP exercises, and beneficiary targeting and registration for the Integrated Resilience Management Programme (IRMP).
  • Facilitated food and cash distribution planning and output monitoring across distribution points, managing beneficiary complaints and community feedback mechanisms.
  • Built capacity of field facilitators and PMCs in integrated watershed management, WASH, soil and water conservation, VSL formation, and gender mainstreaming.
Oct – Dec 2015
Quinda Engineering
Lilongwe, Malawi

Engineer Consultant

Water & Civil Infrastructure
  • Managed full consultancy lifecycle for water and civil infrastructure projects from technical scoping through implementation oversight and client reporting.
  • Conducted structured field site inspections, coordinated with contractors and local authorities, developing adaptive problem-solving competencies consistent with humanitarian M&E requirements.
Jul – Dec 2014
Ministry of Transport & Works, GoM
Lilongwe, Malawi

Materials Engineer

Centre Materials Laboratory
  • Conducted materials testing and quality assurance for government infrastructure projects, maintaining detailed technical reports for government and donor stakeholders.
  • Applied structured field monitoring approaches directly consistent with post-distribution and asset verification monitoring in humanitarian cash and FFA programmes.
Jun – Dec 2013
FISD
Nkhatabay, Malawi

Irrigation Engineer in Training

Community Waterworks
  • Contributed to two community waterworks projects, gaining direct field experience in water infrastructure development underpinning humanitarian food security and resilience programming.
  • Mapped irrigation system installations, engaged community members and local authorities, building participatory engagement skills foundational to CBPP and needs assessment processes.

Key Achievements

USD 10.3M ECHO/WFP Portfolio — Zero audit findings across the full 6-year programme period. On time, on scope, on budget.
13,721 Direct Beneficiaries reached with 100% Post-Distribution Monitoring satisfaction score across all 21 distribution points.
WFP National Replication Model — Domwe-Mposa broke the cycle of recurring hunger within 2 years. Now cited by WFP Malawi as the national reference model.
500% Household Income Growth — Achieved through integrated livelihoods, market linkage programming, and direct connection to PTS and Spar (Ekhaya).
371 VSL Groups Established across target communities for sustained financial inclusion. Plus 13 beekeeping, 3 mushroom, and 4 briquette VSL groups as sustainable IGAs.
122 Ha Irrigation Infrastructure — Engineering design and construction supervision of Bakasala (100ha), Domwe (12ha), and Mposa (10ha) schemes with solar power.
80% Improvement in Clean Water Access — 2 solar irrigation systems (6ha each) + 21 shallow wells developed across programme target areas.
Multi-Programme Coordination — IRMP, FFA, R4 Rural Resilience, and BMZ Nutrition Sensitive Programmes delivered simultaneously under WFP, USAID, DFID, and Germany Cooperation.

Selected Projects

Key humanitarian and development programmes managed and delivered across Malawi from 2016 to present.

ECHOWFPDFIDUSAIDBMZ

Cash Assistance for Asset Creation

📍 Blantyre District, Malawi · 21 Field Distribution Points · 2016–2022

Led delivery across 21 field sites, developing solar irrigation systems, water harvesting infrastructure, and Village Savings and Loan groups. The Domwe-Mposa integrated livelihoods model was adopted by WFP Malawi as a national replication reference for breaking the cycle of recurring hunger within two years.

Bakasala, Domwe & Mposa Irrigation Schemes

📍 Blantyre District, Malawi · Jan–Mar 2017

Engineering design and construction supervision of 122 hectares of community irrigation infrastructure comprising Bakasala (100 ha), Domwe (12 ha), and Mposa (10 ha). This infrastructure forms the physical foundation of the Domwe-Mposa WFP national replication model for breaking the cycle of recurring hunger.

Smallholder Farmers Market Support

📍 Blantyre District, Malawi · 2018–2022

Linked two marketing cooperatives to inclusive and sustainable markets — specifically PTS and Spar (now Ekhaya) — enabling household income growth of up to 500% through market access facilitation, business management training, and value chain integration for smallholder farmers.

Additional Projects & Field Activities

Livelihoods · IGA

Honey Harvesting & Beekeeping Programme

Facilitated 13 beekeeping groups, providing training in honey harvesting and processing at Mposa, GVH Undi. Delivered as sustainable income-generating activity under WFP FFA.

GVH Undi, Blantyre2016–2022
Energy · Climate

Efficient Energy Stove Training

Facilitated briquette-making and efficient energy stove training at GVH Zinganguwo, establishing 4 briquette VSL groups as sustainable IGAs reducing household fuel costs and environmental impact.

GVH Zinganguwo2016–2022
Environment · Livelihoods

Group Fruit Tree Nursery — Machinjiri

Established community fruit tree nurseries in Machinjiri as part of conservation agriculture and environmental restoration activities linked to FFA asset creation programming.

Machinjiri, Blantyre2016–2022
Food Security · Nutrition

Group Vegetable Gardens — Machinjiri

Established group and household vegetable gardens in Machinjiri. Gardens ready for harvest for both household consumption and market selling — directly improving nutrition and income outcomes.

Machinjiri, Blantyre2016–2022
Conservation Agriculture

Sweet Potato & Vine Propagation

Supported farmers in harvesting sweet potato vines for planting as part of conservation agriculture programming, complementing deep trench and swale construction at programme sites.

Blantyre District2016–2022
Watershed · Conservation

Vetiver Nursery Establishment

Established vetiver grass nurseries and planting at GVH Kuntaja for slope stabilization and soil erosion control as part of integrated watershed management under FFA programme.

GVH Kuntaja2016–2022
Watershed Management

Deep Trench & Swale Construction

Constructed deep trenches and swales at programme sites for soil and water conservation — core FFA asset creation activities that formed the physical foundation of the Domwe-Mposa model.

Mposa, Blantyre2016–2017
Conservation Agriculture

Swale Stabilization with Pigeon Pea

Stabilized swales with pigeon pea at programme sites to prevent erosion and provide additional household food and income, integrating soil conservation with nutrition-sensitive farming.

Blantyre District2016–2022
M&E · Water Systems

Solar Pump Irrigation Monitoring

Monitored solar pump performance, irrigation infrastructure condition, and economic status of beneficiaries at GVH Masaka-Machinjiri — assessing programme outcomes on household incomes.

Masaka-Machinjiri2016–2022
